## ~~~ HEARING_IMPAIRMENTS_TEXT ~~~
## Age related hearing loss, wear hearing aids (not severe as can hear without them or using headphones): 1 (0.1%)
## Age related high frequency loss. No aids-just turn the volume up!: 1 (0.1%)
## Age related minor, hearing aids don't help sound separation: 1 (0.1%)
## Auditory Processing Disorder. I don't do anything specific to try and correct it.: 1 (0.1%)
## deaf in left ear.: 1 (0.1%)
## General hearing loss in right ear from being in the Army.: 1 (0.1%)
## Have moderate deafness in right ear.: 1 (0.1%)
## I am a little deaf in my left ear.: 1 (0.1%)
## I am almost deaf in one ear: 1 (0.1%)
## I am deaf for certain frequencies but I wear a hearing aid in each ear and they were in during the study with headphones on as well: 1 (0.1%)
## I am deaf in my left ear SSD (Single sided deafness) after an operation: 1 (0.1%)
## i am older and just hard of hearing: 1 (0.1%)
## I do sometimes wear hearing aids, but the headphones make up for my mild hearing loss, I just turn the volume up a little if there's a problem.: 1 (0.1%)
## i have 15% hearing loss in both my ears from ear infections as a child. i don't need hearing aids at the moment but i do talk a bit loud and turn up the volume so i can comprehend what im hearing: 1 (0.1%)
## I have a bad case of getting ear infections way more than a typical person, so my ear drums are always healing but I fortunately can still hear well, just sometimes my ear drums are sore.: 1 (0.1%)
## I have a hard time hearing when around a large group of people.  I actually took a hearing test last year, and I have some impairment: 1 (0.1%)
## I have a moderate hearing loss and I wear hearing aids to correct it, listening to music I just adjust the volume: 1 (0.1%)
## I have a really hard time hearing people if there is any other noise in the room.: 1 (0.1%)
## I have had a significant loss of hearing since i was a kid, but it definitely did not affect this study i heared everything very clear: 1 (0.1%)
## I have slight hearing loss in my Right ear.: 1 (0.1%)
## i have some earwax blockage in my left ear. Currently going to doctor to get it removed: 1 (0.1%)
## I have some nerve damage which makes it hard to understand what people are saying.: 1 (0.1%)
## I have Tinnitus.  No measures are being taken.: 1 (0.1%)
## I was born with holes in my eardrums, I have had tympanoplasty surgeries to fix this (3 operations between both ears). My hearing levels are good but my hearing is better in my right ear, than in my left. I struggle sometimes in conversations with lots of people to hear every word of what someone is saying.: 1 (0.1%)
## I wear a hearing aid for help with conversation, but I am able to hear music as clearly as I ever have (with the exception of some high frequency loss).: 1 (0.1%)
## I wear a hearing aid. My right ear needs a minute bit of power and my left ear needs a lot, but they are both corrected with hearing aids.: 1 (0.1%)
## I wear hearing aids due to work related hearing loss.: 1 (0.1%)
## I wear hearing aids for mild to medium hearing loss: 1 (0.1%)
## I'm partially deaf in my right ear. I occasionally wear a hearing aid when the environment is very loud.: 1 (0.1%)
## left ear 60% hearing loss caused by vagus nerve damage.  slight right ear hearing loss caused by the same Vagus nerve damage: 1 (0.1%)
## left ear moderate to severe loss, right ear slight loss.. just started wearing hearing aids: 1 (0.1%)
## loss in right ear. can hear thru earbuds: 1 (0.1%)
## Loss of hearing from occupational noise. Mild.: 1 (0.1%)
## loss of upper range hearing, cannot afford hearing aids although they are recommended. so i read lips as much as possible. : 1 (0.1%)
## Mild age related hearing loss. Had no problems hearing the music.: 1 (0.1%)
## Mild hardness of hearing, no corrections: 1 (0.1%)
## Mild hearing loss.: 1 (0.1%)
## mild impairment, corrected by hearing aids. Fine with louder sounds and headphones: 1 (0.1%)
## moderate bilateral hearing loss - wear hearing aids: 1 (0.1%)
## only 20% hearing in my right ear: 1 (0.1%)
## Otosclerosis - limited hearing in one ear. : 1 (0.1%)
## Otosclerosis. I wear hearing aids. My phone and this laptop are connected to them via bluetooth.: 1 (0.1%)
## Palastasious eustation tube, meaning I cant hear low tones well  - no corrective measures: 1 (0.1%)
## partial hearing in left ear: 1 (0.1%)
## Partial hearing loss - managed by wearing hearing aids.: 1 (0.1%)
## partial hearing loss in my left ear: 1 (0.1%)

MIMC Analysis

Overall MIMC Frequency

## 
## ~~~ OVERALL MIMC FREQUENCY ~~~
## Total valid trials: 17849
## Total MIMCs (thought reports): 14099
## Total no-MIMCs: 3750
## Overall MIMC rate: 78.99 %
## Trials with missing/invalid response: 11
## 
## Thought report word count:
##   Mean:   17.17 
##   Median: 15 
##   SD:     10.58 
##   Min:    1 
##   Max:    111

(optional run) No thought reason

uncomment out the below code to read the no-MIMC responses (fair warning: it’s long)

## 
## ~~~ MIMC FREQUENCY BY GENRE x AGE GROUP ~~~
## # A tibble: 10 × 5
##    genre_code `18to30` `31to45` `46to60` `61to75`

MIMC Ratings Analysis

Overall MIMC Ratings

## 
## ~~~ OVERALL MIMC RATING CHARACTERISTICS ~~~
## Q1: Music-related rating
##   To what degree do you think these thoughts were prompted by the music you just heard?
##       not at all prompted by the music  1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5  entirely prompted by the music
## Mean: 4.58
## SD: 0.69
## Median: 5
## Range: 1 - 5
## Q2: Spontaneous rating
##   Please rate the degree to which you deliberately brought these thoughts to mind
##   versus they came to mind spontaneously.
##       entirely deliberately  1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5  entirely spontaneously
## Mean: 4.02
## SD: 1.06
## Median: 4
## Range: 1 - 5
## Q3: Unreality rating
##   To what degree were these thoughts memories of things you’ve seen or experienced before,
##   versus things you’ve newly imagined?
##       not at all prompted by the music  1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5  entirely prompted by the music
## Mean: 2.65
## SD: 1.33
## Median: 3
## Range: 1 - 5
